title: Understand: The 27 Amendments
description: Check your understanding of the 27 Amendments to the U.S. Constitution.
topic_path: Civil Dialogue, The Constitution, and Constitutional Interpretation

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which THREE amendments deal with due process, the right to a jury trial, and the rights of the accused?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Sixth Amendment provides what THREE rights for criminal defendants?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Seventh Amendment protects which right?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the right to a trial by jury in civil cases
the right to keep and bear arms
the right to petition the government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which amendment responded to James Madison’s concern that a written bill of rights might undermine other rights retained by the people?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Third Amendment protects citizens from being forced by the government to house which of these groups in private homes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which amendment protects those accused of crimes from [[☃ definition 1]]?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: providing information that suggests you are guilty of a crime

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the phrases that best complete the sentence.**

The Seventeenth Amendment changed the process for electing U.S. senators from \_\_\_\_ to \_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: direct election; appointment by state legislatures
appointment by state legislatures; direct election
presidential appointment; vote in the House of Representatives

assessment_items.question_content:
**After the ratification of the Twenty-Third Amendment in 1961, people who lived in which location were granted votes in the Electoral College?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**There has been only one time in U.S.history when an amendment was specifically repealed by another amendment. This happened in 1933, when the Twenty-First Amendment repealed which amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Takings Clause, which protects private property from being taken by the government for public use without just compensation, is found in which amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**During the Progressive Era (1913–1920), Congress ratified amendments related to which issues?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congressional power to establish a federal income tax

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Twenty-Second Amendment [[☃ definition 1]] which tradition established by George Washington?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: included in a system of laws

choices.content: The president must say “So help me God” during the oath of office.
The president must have served in the military.
The president can serve no more than two terms.
The president cannot be a supporter of any political party.
